mechanism_narrative: Sustained year-over-year growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and economic strength, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month rank correlation between INDPRO 12-month growth and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate over three months signals imminent labor market deterioration and economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over the trailing 3 months
failure_condition: In triggered instances, median 20-day SPX return is not less than median of non-triggered instances (Mann-Whitney U p > 0.05) or hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the real fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation) reduces the discount rate on equities and encourages risk-taking, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between the real rate change and 20-day SPX returns is positive (indicating opposite direction) over any 2-year window, or rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, an outsized monthly increase in real retail sales signals overheating demand that will compress corporate profit margins and trigger profit-taking, resulting in negative short-term equity returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (UNRATE < 5%, FEDFUNDS rising, GDP growth > 2%)
failure_condition: In months triggered by the condition, the median 5-day SPX return is not negative, or the proportion of negative returns is less than 55% over a 5-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strength in the housing sector and consumer confidence, leading the economy and driving higher S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 24-month rank correlation between HOUST 3-month growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is below 0.0 over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls is a strong recessionary signal that directly reduces consumption expectations and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS declines, the 5-day SPX return is positive in more than 55% of cases or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The concurrent combination of rising industrial production and falling unemployment creates a virtuous cycle of strong growth without overheating, amplifying positive S&P 500 forward returns beyond the individual contributions of each series.
failure_condition: The predictive accuracy (rank IC) of the combined signal is not significantly greater than the average of the individual signals over any 3-year rolling window (Diebold-Mariano test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a Fed funds rate hike signals a tightening bias that raises borrowing costs and depresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25 basis points over a 3-month window
failure_condition: When high inflation and rate hike conditions are both met, the median 60-day SPX return is not negative, or the proportion of negative returns is below 55% over a 10-year evaluation window.
